body.title: Guru Loop — Master control for dynamic service management
body.description:
Design and implement a stable, immutable master control loop that manages all rack services dynamically.

**Problem:** Services go down (web_server), must manually restart. Design principle "easy to the point of disappearing" is violated.

**Solution:** Guru Loop (Master Control)
- Stays running, stable, immutable (~300 LOC)
- Health-checks all services
- Auto-launches on demand (if circuit breaker closed)
- Proxies requests to services
- Services pluggable via YAML config
- Circuit breaker for maintenance mode

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint
**Design rules:** system-resilience, dynamic-service-management, graceful-degradation
**Scope boundary:**
  IN: Guru loop core, service registry, health checks, HTTP proxy, circuit breaker
  OUT: Service-specific monitoring, metrics collection, advanced scheduling
**Completion criteria:**
  1. Guru loop spawned and monitored, survives child service crashes
  2. Service registration via YAML config (no code changes to add services)
  3. On-demand launch: request to missing service triggers spawn
  4. Circuit breaker: touch flag to disable service, prevents restart loop
  5. Test: kill web_server, next HTTP request auto-restarts it
  6. All 3+ existing services registered and working under Guru

**Intention:** I intend that any single service can crash or be recycled without affecting the master control loop, and that the system self-heals transparently with circuit breaker control.
